heading: Omitted
label: 635, 636 Omitted
notes: CodificationSections 635 and 636, relating to Territory of Hawaii, were omitted in view of admission of Hawaii into the Union.Section 635, acts , ; , , continued in force all laws relating to judicial departments and procedure, but made certain changes with reference to membership qualifications for membership on juries.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 8331 Stat. 157Apr. 1, 1952, ch. 127, § 166 Stat. 32Section 636, acts , ; , , set out standards for disqualification of jurors who were related by affinity or consanguinity with a person interested in case being tried and for disqualification of judges in certain cases.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 8431 Stat. 157May 27, 1910, ch. 258, § 636 Stat. 447
amendments: CodificationSections 635 and 636, relating to Territory of Hawaii, were omitted in view of admission of Hawaii into the Union.Section 635, acts , ; , , continued in force all laws relating to judicial departments and procedure, but made certain changes with reference to membership qualifications for membership on juries.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 8331 Stat. 157Apr. 1, 1952, ch. 127, § 166 Stat. 32Section 636, acts , ; , , set out standards for disqualification of jurors who were related by affinity or consanguinity with a person interested in case being tried and for disqualification of judges in certain cases.Apr. 30, 1900, ch. 339, § 8431 Stat. 157May 27, 1910, ch. 258, § 636 Stat. 447
